notes: Game crashing occasionally, otherwise it runs fine
title: Mass Effect 2

notes: After updating to nvidia 415 driver and Proton 3-16-6 no issues at all w/ launching. This may be because I have launched it before. Beware the sound issue, just close and restart game if it happens. Just wanted to come in and give this game a Plat review.
title: Mass Effect 2

notes: Despite enabling audio in launcher, no audio on first game start but fine on second start. Some fps drops throughout. (Completed game previously with wine and essentially the same performance running the game with proton so far).
title: Mass Effect 2

notes: Gameplay generally works fine. Motion Blur needs to be disabled in the ME configuration tool in order for some parts of the game to work/render (ie Shadow Broker taxi chase). Game regularly crashes with a Pure Virtual Function error, particularly common on loading screens, so save often to not lose progress (ME1 has same issue), gets very frustrating the longer you try to play.

notes: Got through the first few missions. Changed resolution. Performance good at max settings. Runs like native.
title: Mass Effect 2

notes:
Mass effect 2 was unplayable for me with hitching/freezing every few seconds on  Proton/Wine 4.x but works very well for me with Proton 3.7-8 and steam launch options: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%.

But after that it ran perfectly like it was native from Windows. Some PCs I had this unplayable stuttering issue on proton 4.x, some I didn't. My systemd was above 240.
title: Mass Effect 2

notes:
Installs and runs great right out of the box. Used the configurator that comes with the game to set max visuals and launched right into the game and started a new game. 

Played through a few missions, no glitches or hic-ups.
title: Mass Effect 2

notes:
The game mostly works, but: 1) The performance is much worse than is to be expected from a game with this level of graphics, and 2) the game sometimes crashes (every 2 or 3 hours or so).

Also the launcher doesn't exit on exiting and has to be killed, running the game from the launcher sometimes results in no sound and the launcher doesn't correctly identify the size of the video memory. Using protontricks to set the correct vidmem size doesn't seem to result in better performance tough.

All in all: playable, but with issues.
title: Mass Effect 2

notes: I play it with "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%" to enable D9VK for a more stable performance. Runs perfectly. The launcher indeed needs to be killed, but its only use to set graphical settings and import ME1 saves.
title: Mass Effect 2

notes:
sound only worked correctly after second run as others have mentioned. Had to copy save files for import with this command, then use launcher utility to import.

cp -r ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/17460/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My\ Documents/BioWare/Mass\ Effect/Save/ .
title: Mass Effect 2

notes: settings: PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 PROTON_NO_D3D10=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1; somtimes heavy frame drops / lag with proton 4.11-3, but 3.7-8 works perfectly fine
title: Mass Effect 2

notes: Works really well so far. The only issue I had was being unable to connect to Cerberus Network, but I think it's a problem with EA servers and has nothing to do with Proton.

notes: Sound only work once I ran it for the third time, so some reason, otherwise, pretty flawless, so far.
